assets.description: Hash-named self-hosted subsets referenced from entry CSS — skipped: fonts already shipped via vendored @nimiq/style fonts.css

notes: Key mechanics learned: (1) ipx originals at /_ipx/_/, but several prismic files ONLY resolve at the bare /assets/images/prismic/ path and vice versa — my downloader tried both and verified with file(1) since nimiq.com 404s return 200-sized 5030-byte HTML shells that masquerade as images. (2) The richest haul was NOT tags but data-URI SVGs in /_nuxt/entry.DNS3Ce9T.css: 165 UnoCSS icons including the full i-nimiq set, 41 hexagonal country flags, dotted continent cutouts matching the hero map style, and the honeycomb band source. (3) /staking-calculator/ is a separate legacy Vue app with its own assets. (4) /albatross block diagrams are 404 in production — nimiq.com ships broken images there. (5) gods-light.webp confirmed as the literal hero sky (alt='Nimiq Gods Rays Background', blur-60 overlay). Duplicates with the parallel brand sweep (favicon, og.png, github awesome artwork) were detected via references/assets/brand/SOURCES.txt and removed. Nothing exceeded 3MB; no videos encountered.

notes: Runtime check of wallet.nimiq.com confirmed the deployed app serves exactly the clone's assets (consensus-sprite, account-ring.png, staking-preview-icon, sharing-banner og:image, favicon set, self-hosted fonts) — no extra runtime-only assets found in the entry CSS/JS bundles; lazy-route chunk assets all exist in the local clone. Wallet does NOT use ipx; assets are webpack-hashed under /img/. Biggest registry wins: (1) original consensus-sprite.svg — consensus-icon currently inlines a recreation; (2) original swap-change-background.svg + horizontal-double-arrow.svg for swap-balance-bar; (3) the 6 official PWA screenshots as ground-truth references for pixel verification; (4) the exact self-hosted Muli/FiraMono woff files; (5) staking modal art (staking-graph, estimated-rewards-projection) for any future staking-modal component. Skipped: square-country-simple-flags.zip (11.6MB) and the ~250 individual flag SVGs (generic third-party), 70 Vue icon components (cataloged, inline SVG in .vue files). Duplicate favicon.ico kept only at img/favicon.ico.

assets.name: vue-components Icons.ts icon set (NOT downloaded — source refs only)
assets.description: ~50 Vue icon components (alert-circle, arrows, cashlink x3 sizes, checkmark, copy, etc.) — all import directly from @nimiq/style/src/icons/*.svg; no binary assets live in vue-components itself. QrCode.vue uses qr-creator lib (procedural), QrScanner uses qr-scanner worker (no images).
assets.coveredByUs: vendored already — @nimiq/style icons in registry assets/css

notes: Big find: the runtime Iqons sprite. hub's vue config inlines it via file-loader as /img/iqons.min.a593173e.svg (NOT at site root — root path returns the SPA fallback HTML with a fake 200; Content-Type check required). Saved hash-stripped as iqons.min.svg so registry/components/identicon can point Iqons.svgPath at a local file instead of jsdelivr. Second confirmation of good prior work: swap-change-background.svg is already byte-equivalent inline in our swap-balance-bar component. hub has no src/assets dir — everything lives in public/; the only runtime-only asset (not in the repo's public/) is the iqons sprite, which webpack copies out of node_modules/@nimiq/iqons at build time. vue-components contains zero image files (icons all come from @nimiq/style, which we already vendor). Skipped checkout-demo.mp4 (video) and gstatic Muli fonts (already vendored via @nimiq/style).
source: upstream/hub + upstream/vue-components local clones + https://hub.nimiq.com runtime (HTML, CSS, JS bundles probed for hashed assets)
LOCAL upstream/nimiq-style + upstream/nimiq-ui (packages/nimiq-icons, nimiq-css, nimiq-maplibre-styles, docs/public)

assets.name: nimiq-style-icons/ (39 svgs + _FORMAT.md)
assets.description: Legacy @nimiq/style icon set as individual stroke/fill SVGs (arrow-left, hexagon, qr-code, ledger, cashlink, view/view-off, etc.) plus the _FORMAT.md authoring spec (24x24 grid rules). Verified identical coverage to the sprite: symbol ids in nimiq-style.icons.svg == filenames, 39/39.
assets.coveredByUs: vendored already as sprite at assets/css/legacy/nimiq-style.icons.svg; individual files were NOT in assets/ until now

assets.name: nimiq-icons/icons.json (+info,metadata,chars)
assets.description: THE modern Nimiq icon distribution: Iconify JSON set, prefix 'nimiq', 323 icons = 186 plain UI icons (12x12 stroke, 2px round caps) + 48 'duotone-*' marketing illustrations + 89 'logos-*'. Built from Figma via @iconify/tools (scripts/index.ts); published as npm 'nimiq-icons' v1.0.0-beta.162; consumed through nimiq-css unocss preset as i-nimiq:* classes. No spritesheet/loose-SVG distribution exists upstream.
assets.coveredByUs: not covered — registry components hand-inline a handful of icon paths (e.g. payment-info-line, hero-section); the set itself was never vendored

assets.name: extracted-logos/ (89 standalone svgs)
assets.description: Official logo family extracted to standalone SVGs with gradient defs intact: logos-nimiq{,-horizontal,-vertical,-white-*,-mono} radial-gradient hexagon + wordmark (76x18 horizontal), Nimiq Pay, Nimiq Wallet, CryptoPayment Link (cpl-horizontal/tag/white), Crypto Map, Cryptocity, Developer Center, Nimiq Forum, Frankenmint (fm-*), Super Simple Swap, shiny-nim coin, multisig, gmaps-pin, currency coins (bitcoin, lightning-bitcoin, ethereum, usdc, usdt, euro/usd/colones outline) and social marks (discord, github, twitter, telegram, instagram, facebook, reddit, youtube) — each in color + mono variants.
assets.coveredByUs: hand-recreated in hero-section — its meta.json literally says the CPL / coin / ring logos are 'simplified placeholder marks'; real marks now available

assets.name: extracted-duotone/ (48 standalone svgs)
assets.description: Two-tone marketing illustration icons used across nimiq.com feature sections: duotone-cash-register, btc-invoice, htlc-box, high-five, handshake, incognito, globe, fiat-exchange, crypto-currencies-btc-eth/usdc, cryptos-pizza, block-stairs, gamepad, dashboard, etc.

assets.name: nimiq-flags/icons.json (+info,metadata,chars)
assets.description: Iconify JSON set 'nimiq-flags': 422 hexagon-cropped country/region flags (32x32, e.g. de-hexagon, us-hexagon, au-nsw-hexagon), npm package 'nimiq-flags'. Used by Crypto Map / wallet country pickers.

assets.name: docs-assets/favicon.svg (+ico, 16/32 png)
assets.description: Official Nimiq hexagon logo mark as gradient SVG, 295x263 viewBox with radial-gradient gold fill — clean standalone source of the hexagon mark, plus rendered ico/png favicon set.

assets.name: docs-assets/scripto.webp
assets.description: 'Scripto' mascot illustration from the nimiq-ui docs, 375KB webp raster.

assets.name: docs-assets/nimiq-style-favicon.ico
assets.description: Legacy @nimiq/style demo favicon (15KB ico, hexagon mark, 2018-era).

assets.name: fonts/mulish-*.woff2 (10 files)
assets.description: Mulish brand-font woff2 unicode-range subsets (hashed vitepress build copies, ~25-50KB each). Relevant gap: our assets/css/modern/fonts.css declares Mulish + Fira Mono @font-face pointing at /assets/fonts/*.ttf which we never vendored — these are the only real font binaries in either upstream.

assets.name: maplibre/nimiq-maplibre-light.json
assets.description: Nimiq-branded MapLibre GL style JSON (88KB, light theme) — the official map styling used by Crypto Map; directly relevant to the hero world-map work that triggered this sweep.

assets.name: FiraMono-400.ttf / Mulish-VariableFont_wght.ttf (NOT present upstream)
assets.description: Canonical font binaries that nimiq-css fonts.css expects consumers to ship; not included in either upstream repo — would need to be fetched from a live nimiq site or Google Fonts. Cataloged as a known gap, nothing downloaded.

assets.name: firacode-*.woff2 (skipped)
assets.description: Fira Code subsets used only by the vitepress docs code blocks (brand monospace is Fira Mono, not Fira Code) — skipped as non-brand.

assets.name: claude.svg / openai.svg (skipped)
assets.description: Third-party AI-vendor marks in the vitepress theme's copy-as-markdown widget; not Nimiq brand assets — skipped.

notes: Key answers: (1) modern nimiq-icons is distributed ONLY as Iconify JSON npm packages ('nimiq-icons' prefix nimiq, 'nimiq-flags' prefix nimiq-flags), generated from Figma frames icons/duotone/logos/flags by scripts/index.ts — no spritesheet or loose SVGs upstream, so I copied the JSON sets AND extracted logos-* (89) + duotone-* (48) into standalone SVGs with gradient defs verified intact. (2) The 'logos' subcategory inside icons.json is the highest-value find — real CryptoPayment Link / Nimiq Pay / coin / social marks that hero-section currently fakes with placeholder paths. (3) nimiq-style individual icons == our vendored sprite exactly (diffed symbol ids vs filenames), so that gap is only about per-file convenience. (4) Gap flagged: assets/css/modern/fonts.css @font-faces point to /assets/fonts/*.ttf we do not ship; Mulish woff2 subsets copied as interim. Total saved: 206 files, 2.5MB, all under 3MB each.

source: Nimiq brand/media resources beyond the main site: github.com/nimiq/designs (official logo pack), github.com/nimiq/awesome (app/exchange icons hotlinked by nimiq.com), github.com/nimiq/identicons, nimiq.com favicon/og-image. nimiq.com/brand, /press, /media-kit, /media, /presskit all 404 (no public media-kit page exists). github.com/nimiq/website is private (404).

assets.name: logo-pack/ (25 SVGs, incl. nimiq-logo.min.svg)
assets.description: THE official Nimiq logo pack: horizontal + vertical wordmark and hexagon signet (16/32/64/base optical sizes) in colored (radial gold gradient #ec991c->#e9b213), white-text-colored-signet, all-black, all-white. Flattened to logo-pack/{colored,black,white}/. This repo ('Nimiq Designs and Assets') is the canonical brand source nobody had found.
assets.coveredByUs: not covered — no nimiq_logo*.svg anywhere in repo; upstream/ vendors nimiq-style icons but NOT the logo pack

assets.name: logo-pack/colored/_extended/{nimiq_keyguard,nimiq_miner,nimiq_safe,nimiq_shop}.svg
assets.description: Official 'extended' product logos: hexagon signet variants for Keyguard (key cutout), Miner, Safe, Shop — legacy product identity set.

assets.name: identicons/src-svg/ (84 SVGs in bottom/face/side/top)
assets.description: The named identicon PART SOURCES (face/unicorn.svg, bottom/robot.svg, side/wings.svg, top/crown.svg, ...) that compile into the iqons sprite — lets us render/debug identicons offline and by part name instead of treating the CDN sprite as a black box.
assets.coveredByUs: hand-recreated in identicon component via @nimiq/iqons@1.6.0 jsdelivr CDN at runtime; sources not vendored

assets.name: identicons/identicons.min.svg
assets.description: Compiled 88KB symbol sprite (face_01..., side_..., bottom_..., top_...) — same content the @nimiq/iqons CDN bundle ships; drop-in for offline identicon rendering.
assets.coveredByUs: identicon component fetches the equivalent from CDN; now vendored

assets.name: awesome-apps/nim-world.svg
assets.description: 218x218 'NIM World' globe illustration — white landmasses on blue circle with gold NIM hexagons; hotlinked directly by nimiq.com's apps section (confirmed in homepage HTML).

assets.name: awesome-apps/nimiq-hexagon.svg
assets.description: 325-byte canonical gold-gradient Nimiq hexagon signet, the exact file nimiq.com hotlinks as a generic app icon.
assets.coveredByUs: hand-recreated in app-showcase-card (gold hexagon chip) and honeycomb-band

assets.name: awesome-apps/ (25 more: crypto-payment-logo.svg, nimiq-wallet.svg, staking-calculator.svg, nimiq-pay.webp, crypto-map.webp, multisig-preview.svg, nimiq-2048.svg, nimiq-sunset-cyberspace.svg, nimiq-tip-bot.svg, trust-wallet.svg, nimtris.svg, nimiq-gift-card.svg, nimiq-shop.svg, nimiq-fm.svg, nimiq-watch.svg, nim-stalker.svg, nimiq-game-store.svg, nimiq-multisig-wallet.svg, paynim-telegram-bot.svg, accept-donations.svg, staking ... + nimiqscan.png 834KB, nimiq-cafe.png, pixels_logo.png, re-nim.png, twini-app.svg)
assets.description: Full ecosystem app-icon set that nimiq.com's 'THE APPS' grid hotlinks from raw.githubusercontent.com (6 confirmed in live homepage HTML: nim-world, nimiq-2048, nimiq-hexagon, nimiq-sunset-cyberspace, nimiq-tip-bot, trust-wallet). multisig-preview.svg (158KB) is the same multisig promo art nimiq.com serves via ipx prismic.
assets.coveredByUs: not covered — app-showcase-card fakes icons with CSS

assets.name: awesome-exchanges/ (16 logos)
assets.description: Exchange logo set used on the Buy-NIM/exchanges page: ascendex, changehero, changelly, coinex, coinspot, coinswitch, gate.io, hitbtc, kriptomat, kucoin, mexc, simpleswap, stealthex, swapspace, swapzone (SVG) + coinpay.png.

assets.name: awesome-mini-apps/ (3 SVGs)
assets.description: Mini-app icons: albermonte-evm-mini-wallet, eligioo-nimtris, onmax-hex-hopper.

assets.name: awesome-data-nimiq-apps.json
assets.description: Machine-readable app index (name/description/logo URL/link) that drives nimiq.com's apps grid — maps every icon above to its app. Sibling dist files exist (nimiq-exchanges.json, nimiq-explorers.json, nimiq-mini-apps.json, nimiq-resources.json, rpc-servers.json) — not downloaded.

assets.name: nimiq-com-meta/og.png
assets.description: nimiq.com Open Graph share card, 1200x600 PNG RGBA (824KB). Note: bare nimiq.com URL 301s — must fetch www with -L.

assets.name: nimiq-com-meta/favicon.ico
assets.description: nimiq.com favicon, multi-size ICO (48/32/16, 32-bit). This is the ONLY favicon convention the site has — apple-touch-icon.png, favicon.svg, favicon-{16,32}.png, site.webmanifest, manifest.json, android-chrome-* all 404.

assets.name: logo CMYK print files (SKIPPED)
assets.description: Print-grade EPS + JPEG of every logo variant, ~600KB each (~20MB total) — skipped as print-only; RGB SVGs cover all design use. RGB PNG @1x/2x/3x rasters also skipped (redundant with SVGs).

assets.name: nimiq-style.icons.svg + src/icons/*.svg (NOT re-downloaded)
assets.description: 40-icon UI sprite (arrows, cashlink, checkmark, hexagon, qr-code, ledger, ...).

assets.name: prismic partner wordmarks on nimiq.com (NOT downloaded — main-site sweep scope)
assets.description: Seen in homepage HTML, originals via /_ipx/_/: aUQvvnNY..._cryptopayment-link-logo-white.svg (white CryptoPayment Link wordmark, 220x32), aUQv83NY..._polygon-logo.svg (32x32), aUQv2XNY..._btc-lightning-logo-white.svg (43x31), aUQvvHNY..._naka-logo-white.svg (84x20), plus product shots NimiqWalletPreview.webp (1734x1164), NimiqPayPreview.webp (490x916), CPLPreview.webp (910x1268), multisig-preview.svg (673x422, duplicate of awesome copy), gods-light.webp (1600x900 hero bg).
assets.coveredByUs: not covered — flagged for the nimiq-com sweep agent (references/assets/nimiq-com/ was empty)

assets.name: identicons demo/catalog pages (SKIPPED)
assets.description: Part-catalog and builder demo pages plus an svg.archive/ of retired identicon parts (balloon, kraken, dracula, panda, ...) — archive parts never render in current identicons, skipped; src-svg active set captured instead.

notes: Headline find: github.com/nimiq/designs is the canonical 'Nimiq Designs and Assets' repo containing the full official logo pack (wordmark + signet, 3 colorways, 4 optical sizes, SVG/PNG/EPS) — this repo had zero copies of the official logo. Second find: nimiq.com hotlinks its apps-section icons LIVE from raw.githubusercontent.com/nimiq/awesome (same pattern as the map.svg discovery — real shipped art, not site-local), so the awesome repo IS a nimiq.com asset source. Third: nimiq/identicons src/svg gives the 84 named part sources behind the iqons CDN sprite our identicon component depends on; we now have an offline sprite + parts. Confirmed there is NO public brand/press/media-kit page on nimiq.com (all 404) and nimiq/website is private. All downloads verified (file magic checked); total 159 files / 2.8MB in references/assets/brand/ with provenance in SOURCES.txt. Gotchas for future sweeps: bare nimiq.com 301s (use www + -L for og.png); zsh chokes on unquoted '===' echo args.
